objective: Adversarial review of the plan for cross-cutting integration tests, naming guard, mcp-llm docs, and the 0.3.0 version bump closing sprint S02.
key_changes: Every load-bearing symbol verified on disk: ScriptedClient, corrective_refusal, is_in_toolset, build_full_toolset, run_explore, ExploreAnswer.turns all exist.
AC1a: non-TTY guard string 'interactive terminal' confirmed at cli/src/config_tui/mod.rs:39.
AC1b: write_mcp_json_explore uses object-key overwrite (MCP_SERVER_KEY) with args ['serve','--explore'] — dedup claim correct.
AC1b: mcp_llm_steering_block_idempotency (cli.rs:588) and mcp_llm_setup helper (cli.rs:541) exist — plan correctly avoids duplication and reuses setup.
AC1c: distinct from shell_binary_not_in_allowlist_is_refused (dispatch path) — tests loop-level is_in_toolset->corrective_refusal gating via allowed_tools. Not redundant.
AC2: naming-guard scan scope excludes cli/tests/ so the test's own literal cannot self-trip; anchors to CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/..
Non-TTY test correctly avoids Command::output() hang via spawn+poll+kill with 5s deadline.
All 5 ACs mapped to concrete tests/doc edits; version bump touches 4 files + Cargo.lock per RELEASING.md.
Advisory: confirm answer.turns==1 off-by-one; keep AC1c allowlist angle explicit; prefer std walk over ignore::Walk; record AC4 checklist pass in PR desc.
